proposed_description: Verbs whose governed preposition before an infinitive is a: cominciare, imparare, provare, riuscire, aiutare, continuare.
rationale: 16 items land on the single verb_governed leaf, the heaviest concentration in the batch. The diagnostically distinct patterns (reaches for the wrong family before an infinitive; drops the preposition before an object, the bisogno-calque) deserve separate histories. Items currently cite the parent leaf so nothing strict-rejects; external_id prefixes (prep_vg_a_, prep_vg_di_, prep_vg_obj_) make later migration mechanical.

proposed_description: Verbs whose governed preposition before an infinitive is di: finire, cercare, decidere, smettere, dimenticare.
rationale: 16 items land on the single verb_governed leaf, the heaviest concentration in the batch. proposed_description: Verbs that fix a preposition before a noun or pronoun object: pensare a, credere a, parlare di, avere bisogno di, innamorarsi di.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: Penso a te; ho bisogno di aiuto

rationale: 16 items land on the single verb_governed leaf, the heaviest concentration in the batch. The diagnostically distinct patterns (reaches for the wrong family before an infinitive; drops the preposition before an object, the bisogno-calque) deserve separate histories.
